checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains only text found in the source PDF. The metadata title and table structure are consistent with the document's content. No fabricated introductions, conclusions, or body text were identified.
The source document does not contain any phone numbers, email addresses, or physical addresses. The JSON output reflects this accurately and does not introduce any fabricated contact information.

All three pages of the source document are represented in the JSON output. Section 1 covers the introductory text, goals, objectives, and responsibilities from Page 1. Sections 2 and 3 capture the multi-page table containing tasks and objectives from Pages 2 and 3. The footer note on the final page is also included.

The table in the source document spans two pages. Page 2 contains 8 task rows (2.1 to 4.3) and Page 3 contains 7 task rows (4.4 to 4.10), totaling 15 data rows. The JSON output splits this into two table elements (indices 20 and 26) which correctly contain 8 rows and 7 rows respectively, plus header rows. No data rows are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The core programmatic content of the work plan—including purpose, goals, objectives, and specific task details—is fully preserved. While repetitive page headers ('NC GICC', 'FY 2019-2020') and page numbering ('Page X of 3') are omitted, this is standard for semantic document conversion and does not constitute a significant text omission of the document's informative content.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: A thorough comparison of specific data points—such as dates (e.g., 6/2021 for task 3.2, 11/2019 for task 4.3), names (e.g., John Bridgers, Anna Verrill), and task descriptions—shows that the JSON output maintains precise wording and meaning consistent with the source PDF.

checks.a_analysis: The page headers 'Work Plan' and 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee' appear on every page of the source document and are correctly captured once per page-representing section in the JSON output. No unauthorized duplication of paragraphs or text blocks was found. The duplication is inherent to the source's layout.

The document structure is sound. The document title 'Work Plan' is set as H1. Major sections (Goals, Objectives, etc.) are H2. Sub-headings like the table descriptive title in section 2 are H3. The hierarchy is logical and follows a consistent structure without skipping levels (e.g., H1 -> H2 -> H3).

The tables spanning pages 2 and 3 are correctly identified as individual table elements within their respective sections. The header row correctly identifies the five columns (Objective number and task number, Lead Member, Date Initiated, End Date, Status). Data cells are properly aligned with their respective headers, and multiline cell content from the source is correctly aggregated into single strings.

The reading order in the JSON follows the source document's flow from top to bottom and page to page correctly. Content sections such as Goals, Objectives, Benefits, Responsibilities, and the Task table appear in the intended sequence as presented in the original PDF.

On page 1, the source document uses alpha-labeled (A, B, C) and numbered (1, 2, 3, 4) lists for Goals, Objectives, Expected Benefits, and Ongoing Responsibilities. The JSON output converts all of these to 'unordered' lists and the original numbering/lettering prefixes are removed from the text content of the list items. This results in a loss of the specific sequence and marker style defined in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Goals A. Geospatial datasets... Objectives 1. Communicate... Expected Benefits by Objective 1. The GICC... Ongoing Responsibilities 1. Members...

checks.a_analysis: There are no hyperlinks present in the source document. No fabricated or broken links were introduced into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the visual organization and logical flow of the source PDF. The sequence of headings, introductory text, bulleted lists for goals and objectives, and data tables is maintained correctly. The addition of a Table of Contents sidebar is a standard accessibility improvement for HTML and does not detract from the original layout's intent.

The tables from pages 2 and 3 of the source PDF are correctly rendered in the HTML. They maintain a clear grid structure with borders, distinct headers (styled with a grey background), and properly aligned cell content. Each row and column corresponds accurately to the source material, ensuring the data remains legible and understandable.

The source PDF contains no meaningful images such as logos, photos, or diagrams. The document is strictly text and table-based. The HTML output correctly represents this content without missing or broken visual elements.

No body content (paragraphs, list items, or table rows) is duplicated within the HTML output. Each unique data point from the source PDF appears only once in the body of the converted document. Navigation headings in the sidebar are expected and do not constitute content duplication.
